name: Litoral Trail
name_original: Parque Natural de las Dunas de Liencres – running circular
description: An easy 2.8-mile (4.5 km) jogging loop through the Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres, featuring beaches and pine forests.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Pinar de Liencres traverse a diverse landscape characterized by a dense pine and eucalyptus forest, extensive dune systems, and dramatic coastal cliffs. The region features kilometric beaches such as Valdearenas and Canallave, offering long stretches for running. The Mogro Estuary adds to the varied terrain, where the Pas River meets the Cantabrian Sea. This combination of woodlands, dunes, and coastline provides varied running surfaces and scenic backdrops. Best jogging routes around Pinar de Liencres The most popular jogging route is Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres – running circular, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes 31 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the unique dune system and surrounding natural park. Another top favourite among local runners is View of Picota-Tolío – Bench With View of Monte Tolío loop from Urbanización El Mirador de Liencres, a moderate 3.8 miles (6.1 km) path. This route offers views of the surrounding hills and coastline. Local runners also love the Bench With View of Monte Tolío – View of the River at Sunset loop from Boo de Piélagos, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with river views, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Jogging around Pinar de Liencres is defined by pine forests, extensive dune systems, and coastal paths along dramatic cliffs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance runs. The routes in Pinar de Liencres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1500 runners have used komoot to explore Pinar de Liencres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Pinar de Liencres?
A: Pinar de Liencres offers a wide variety of running options, with over 100 routes available. These range from easy loops to more challenging long-distance runs, catering to different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What makes Pinar de Liencres a unique place for jogging?
A: The region is characterized by a unique blend of landscapes, including a dense pine and eucalyptus forest, extensive dune systems, and dramatic coastal cliffs. Runners can enjoy diverse terrains, from the soft ground of the Pinar to sandy beaches and scenic coastal paths, all within the Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres and Costa Quebrada.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, Pinar de Liencres has several easy routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999093348/natural-park-of-the-dunes-of-liencres-running-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres – running circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.5 km long and explores the unique dune system and surrounding natural park. Approximately 13 of the available routes are rated as easy.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails?
A: You can expect a varied terrain. The pine forest offers soft, cushioned ground from pine needles and sand, ideal for joint-friendly running. Additionally, there are extensive sandy beaches like Valdearenas and Canallave, and coastal paths along dramatic cliffs, providing diverse surfaces and scenic backdrops for your run.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views or interesting landmarks?
A: Absolutely. Many routes provide breathtaking views. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38213034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Picota-Tolío – Bench With View of Monte Tolío loop from Urbanización El Mirador de Liencres&lt;/a&gt; offers vistas of the surrounding hills and coastline. Elevated points like the Mirador de las Dunas and La Picota also provide expansive views of the dunes, estuary, and coastline.

Q: Can I find long-distance running options in the area?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer runs, there are several options. A notable long-distance trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32119242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bench With View of Monte Tolío – View of the River at Sunset loop from Boo de Piélagos&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 17.1 km and leads through varied terrain with river views. There are 16 routes rated as difficult, often indicating longer distances.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Pinar de Liencres?
A: The running routes in Pinar de Liencres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the pine forest, and the stunning coastal scenery, making it a popular destination for outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Pinar de Liencres are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999093348/natural-park-of-the-dunes-of-liencres-running-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres – running circular&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of an easy loop that explores the park's natural beauty.

Q: Is Pinar de Liencres suitable for running with dogs?
A: The Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres and Costa Quebrada is generally dog-friendly, offering ample space for pets to enjoy the outdoors. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to check for any specific local regulations regarding pet access on beaches or certain trails.

Q: What are the best times of year to go running in Pinar de Liencres?
A: Pinar de Liencres is enjoyable year-round, but the spring and autumn months often offer the most pleasant running conditions with milder temperatures. The dense pine forest provides ample shade, making it comfortable even on warmer days, while the coastal paths can be invigorating in cooler weather.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Mogro Estuary?
A: Yes, some routes will take you near or offer views of the Mogro Estuary, where the Pas River meets the Cantabrian Sea. This estuarine environment adds to the park's rich ecosystem and offers opportunities for birdwatching, especially during winter when numerous migratory waterfowl are visible.

Q: Are there any routes that combine forest and beach running?
A: Absolutely. Pinar de Liencres is ideal for combining forest and beach running. The park's design allows for seamless transitions between the shaded pine and eucalyptus forest and the expansive sandy beaches like Valdearenas and Canallave, offering a diverse and refreshing running experience.
tips: Viewpoint par excellence within the Liencres Dunes Park, with beautiful views from the top of the cliff.

Path that surrounds the cliff along the coast with wonderful views, the only small drawback is that right now if you go a little off the main path these paths that get closer to the cliff are more closed by heather and brambles (without so much problem if you pass with long pants). Other than that, it's worth giving yourself a couple of scratches to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres – running circular trail?
faqs.answer: While the specific starting point for this circular route isn't detailed, the Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres has several parking areas, particularly near the popular beaches like Valdearenas and Canallave. These are good options for accessing trails within the park.

faqs.question: Is this running route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular running route is considered easy and suitable for all skill levels. It covers approximately 4.54 km with a modest 67.5 meters of elevation gain, making it a great option for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this running circular?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll encounter natural paths, areas with soft ground due to pine needles and sand within the Pine Forests of Liencres, and potentially some paved sections. While running directly on the protected dunes is prohibited, you might experience soft sand on accessible beach areas or paths near them.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect breathtaking coastal scenery, including wild beaches like Canallave Beach, dramatic ocean views, and the unique landscape of sand dunes. The route also passes through the refreshing Pine Forests of Liencres and offers enchanting Views towards La Antigua beach.

faqs.question: Does this running circular connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates significant sections of other official trails. Approximately 83% of the circular follows the 'Senda del Litoral', and about 38% uses the 'Acceso Playa de Valdearenas' trail.

faqs.question: Is the Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres – running circular dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed in the Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect the park's fragile ecosystem and wildlife. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions, especially on beaches during certain seasons.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required to access the Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres. It is a public natural park, part of the wider Costa Quebrada, which is a UNESCO Global Geopark, and is freely accessible for visitors to enjoy.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this circular route?
faqs.answer: The park offers a pleasant experience year-round. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer is also popular, with the pine forests offering shade, but it can be busier. Winter offers opportunities for birdwatching and dramatic coastal views, though conditions might be cooler and windier.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for a run on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, comfortable running shoes suitable for both natural paths and potentially soft sand are recommended. Layers are advisable due to coastal breezes. Don't forget water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and perhaps a light jacket, especially if running near sunset.

faqs.question: Can I expect to see any wildlife while running?
faqs.answer: The Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres is known for its rich biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, especially migratory waterfowl in winter, as well as unique flora adapted to the dune environment. Keep an eye out, particularly in the Pine Forests of Liencres and along the coast.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities near the trailhead, like cafes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area around the Natural Park of the Dunes of Liencres, particularly in nearby towns and villages, offers various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and other services where you can refuel after your run.
